title: cornus walteri
intro: Cornus walteri, also known as Walter's dogwood, is a deciduous shrub native to East Asia, characterized by its showy white flowers and red berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and aiding in the treatment of fever and diarrhea. The herbal preparations of Cornus walteri include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make tea, capsules, or other medicinal products.
